title: Oenostachys zambesiacus
taxonomy: Oenostachys zambesiacus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as bromeless broomrape. It is native to Africa and has been found in various countries including Malawi, Zambia, and Zimbabwe. This plant is a hemiparasitic species, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. Oenostachys zambesiacus is a perennial herb that grows up to 60 centimeters in height and has yellow or white flowers. The classification of Oenostachys zambesiacus is further grouped into the family Orobanchaceae, tribe Buchnereae, and genus Oenostachys.
